security_deposit.allowed_deductions_rule: - Permissible deductions: Unpaid rent, damage beyond normal wear and tear, other amounts owed under the lease.
security_deposit.penalty_for_bad_faith_rule: - Penalty: If landlord fails to return deposit within 30 days without legitimate deduction, tenant may recover up to twice the amount wrongfully withheld.
security_deposit.deposit_storage_segregation_rule: - Separate account: Landlord must hold deposit in a banking institution in Kansas; must maintain as a separate account or post bond.
security_deposit.deposit_transfer_on_sale_rule: - Transfer of landlord's interest: New landlord must notify tenant of transfer within 30 days; new landlord becomes responsible for the deposit.
security_deposit.special_rules_local_overrides_note: Local ordinances and special housing programs can add stricter rules.

eviction.nonpayment_notice_type: - Nonpayment of rent notice: 3 days notice to quit in most cases; some leases may require 10 days.
eviction.lease_violation_notice_type: - Material noncompliance: 14 days notice to cure, followed by 14 days to vacate if not cured.
eviction.holdover_notice_type: - Month-to-month termination: 30 days written notice required.
eviction.court_process_summary_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in district court; tenant is served with summons; hearing scheduled within days.
eviction.filing_court_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in district court; tenant is served with summons; hearing scheduled within days.
eviction.service_of_process_rule: - Court process: Landlord files in district court; tenant is served with summons; hearing scheduled within days.

landlord_entry.allowed_entry_reasons_rule: - Entry for repairs: Landlord may enter to make repairs, deliver items, or inspect the premises. - Entry to show: Landlord may enter to show premises to prospective tenants, buyers, or lenders.
landlord_entry.entry_time_window_rule: - Entry times: Entry should be at reasonable times.
landlord_entry.penalty_for_illegal_entry_rule: Varies — confirm remedies (injunction, damages, attorney fees) under state law.

rent_increase.fixed_term_increase_allowed_rule: - Fixed-term leases: Rent cannot be increased during the lease term.
rent_increase.month_to_month_increase_allowed_rule: - Rent increases: No statutory limit on amount or frequency for month-to-month tenancies.
rent_increase.delivery_method_rule: - Notice required: For month-to-month tenancies, at least 30 days advance written notice.
rent_increase.retaliation_protection_note: Retaliation protections may limit rent increases after protected tenant complaints; confirm local/state rules.

habitability.landlord_repair_duty_rule: - Landlord's duty: Comply with applicable building and housing codes; make repairs to keep premises in good condition; maintain structural components, plumbing, heating, electrical systems; provide running water and hot water.
habitability.tenant_termination_for_unrepaired_condit: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the rental agreement if conditions substantially impair habitability.
habitability.code_enforcement_contact_rule: Varies — contact local housing/code enforcement (city/county) for inspections and official repair orders.

sources.title: K.S.A. §§ 58-2553 to 58-2561
extractedQuotes: 1. Repair and deduct: ALLOWED. K.S.A. § 58-2559: After giving landlord 14 days written notice and landlord fails to repair, tenant may have repairs made and deduct costs from rent.

sources.title: K.S.A. §§ 58-2553 to 58-2561
extractedQuotes: 2. Rent withholding: ALLOWED. K.S.A. § 58-2560: After 14 days written notice, tenant may withhold rent if landlord fails to make necessary repairs.

sources.title: K.S.A. §§ 58-2553 to 58-2561
extractedQuotes: 3. Termination: Tenant may terminate the rental agreement if conditions substantially impair habitability.

sources.title: K.S.A. §§ 58-2553 to 58-2561
extractedQuotes: - Retaliatory eviction: PROHIBITED. K.S.A. § 58-2572 prohibits landlords from evicting in retaliation for tenant's complaints or exercise of rights.
